intro: New York City Emergency Management (NYCEM) was founded in 1996, originally as the Mayor's Office of Emergency Management, to serve as the city's central hub for coordinating emergency planning and response. It brings together dozens of city agencies, nonprofit partners, and private-sector stakeholders to prepare for and respond to disasters — from hurricanes and blackouts to extreme heat and public health emergencies — across a city of 8.5 million people, and it runs Notify NYC, the City's free emergency alert system used by more than 1.5 million subscribers.
